title: NodeSlide Reviewable Creation Demo
audience: product leaders evaluating an AI presentation workflow
decision: decide whether reviewable generation is materially safer than one-shot deck creation
prompt:
Create a seven-slide product demo for product leaders evaluating reviewable AI presentation generation. Show the initial brief, structured draft, validation, review-before-accept, trace receipt, editable export, and final decision. Use the supplied journey timestamps. Include a large screenshot or explicit screenshot placeholder with numbered callouts, a before/after comparison, an editable interaction flow, and a trace waterfall. Never claim a screenshot was captured when it was not supplied.

Design direction: Use a cinematic minimal direction: fewer words, larger visual centers, deliberate scale changes, one memorable visual climax, and quiet transition beats. Keep all evidence honest and editable.

attachments.content:
stage,elapsed_seconds,status
brief_submitted,0,complete
structured_draft,31,complete
validation,34,complete
human_accept,52,complete
pptx_export,58,complete
No browser screenshot is included in this frozen evidence pack. The deck must reserve and label the screenshot proof honestly rather than fabricating one.

designDirection.label: Cinematic minimal
designDirection.instruction: Use a cinematic minimal direction: fewer words, larger visual centers, deliberate scale changes, one memorable visual climax, and quiet transition beats. Keep all evidence honest and editable.

trace.summary: The user consented to send the full brief and 2 uploaded data sources to OpenRouter. The named Claude Sonnet 5 model supplied the narrative plan through pi-ai; NodeSlide normalized, persisted, and validated the deck deterministically. Self-critique: 1 pass, clean.
trace.metrics: RUN TIME 1s TOKENS 2 → 4,955 COST $0.0554 VALIDATION Passed
